nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Trussell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname from Middle English trus(s)el(le) ‘bundle, parcel’ (Old French troussel), perhaps for a porter (see Truss ), or from the same word in the sense ‘stamp, mould (for stamping coins)’, for someone who minted coins.
variant of Thrussell, a nickname from Middle English thros(t)el, thrus(t)el ‘song thrush’ (Old English thros(t)le). Compare Thrush .

variants.name: Truss
meanings: English (mainly Essex and Middlesex): metonymic occupational name from Middle English trusse ‘bundle, package’ (Old French trousse), perhaps for a porter. See also Trussell .
Americanized form of Ukrainian Trus: nickname from trus ‘rabbit’, typically applied to someone thought to be a coward.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William de Trussell I was born about 1226, in Billesley, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard De Trussell, was 31 and his mother, Isabel de Malesours, was 24. He had at least 2 sons and 5 daughters with Roesia Pantulf. He died on 7 October 1277, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 52.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William de Trussell I est né environ 1226, à Billesley, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard De Trussell, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Isabel de Malesours, avait 24 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 5 filles avec Roesia Pantulf. Il est décédé le 7 octobre 1277, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 52 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William de Trussell I um 1226 in Billesley, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard De Trussell 31 und seine Mutter Isabel de Malesours 24. Er hatte mit Roesia Pantulf mindestens 2 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 7. Oktober 1277 im Alter von 52 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de Trussell I è nato il circa 1226 nel Billesley, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Richard De Trussell, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Isabel de Malesours, aveva 24 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 5 figlie con Roesia Pantulf. Morì il 7 ottobre 1277, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 52 anni.
